Defined Outcome ETFs
articles.summary: This article provides an overview of Defined Outcome ETFs, also known as Buffered ETFs, which utilize options to offer partial upside participation in risk assets while providing a level of downside protection. The article explains that this downside protection comes with a cap on potential upside. It also notes that existing products typically assume a one-year holding period and provides a comprehensive list of such ETFs, along with various financial metrics and analytical tools.

articles.title: Duos Technologies Group Sets First Quarter 2026 Earnings Call for Monday, May 18, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET
articles.summary: Duos Technologies Group, Inc. (DUOT) will host a conference call on Monday, May 18, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET to discuss its first-quarter financial results for the period ended March 31, 2026. The company will release its results via press release prior to the call. Duos Technologies specializes in modular, colocation Edge and AI data centers and technology infrastructure solutions, focusing on scaling its edge data center platforms.

F5 expands Red Hat partnership with new Kubernetes and AI security tools
articles.summary: F5 Inc. has announced an expanded collaboration with Red Hat to introduce new security solutions for Kubernetes applications and AI workloads. Key offerings include F5 WAF for NGINX on NGINX Gateway Fabric, providing Layer 7 protection for Kubernetes in Red Hat OpenShift environments, and an AI quickstart program for Red Hat OpenShift AI. These initiatives aim to address evolving application security challenges by leveraging open standards and integrating with existing DevOps workflows.
articles.content: F5 Inc. has announced an expanded collaboration with Red Hat to introduce new security solutions for Kubernetes applications and AI workloads. Key offerings include F5 WAF for NGINX on NGINX Gateway Fabric, providing Layer 7 protection for Kubernetes in Red Hat OpenShift environments, and an AI quickstart program for Red Hat OpenShift AI. These initiatives aim to address evolving application security challenges by leveraging open standards and integrating with existing DevOps workflows.

articles.title: This new HPE server packs 64TB memory for real-time business data
articles.summary: HPE has introduced the Compute Scale-up Server 3250, a new server boasting an industry-first 64TB of DDR5 memory and powered by Intel Xeon 6 processors. This server is designed for critical business applications that require large in-memory databases, enabling faster transactions and real-time analytics for workloads like SAP Cloud ERP. HPE emphasizes the server's scalability, multi-layered security, and high availability, building on its leadership in SAP HANA-certified servers.
articles.content: HPE has introduced the Compute Scale-up Server 3250, a new server boasting an industry-first 64TB of DDR5 memory and powered by Intel Xeon 6 processors. This server is designed for critical business applications that require large in-memory databases, enabling faster transactions and real-time analytics for workloads like SAP Cloud ERP. HPE emphasizes the server's scalability, multi-layered security, and high availability, building on its leadership in SAP HANA-certified servers.
